whatHappened: search_replace tool corrupts template literals with embedded newlines - converts escaped \n to literal newlines breaking TypeScript syntax
whyItHappened: Did not use sed/shell tools for files with complex template literals; relied on search_replace which has a known issue with newlines in template strings
prevention: For files with template literals containing \n escapes, use sed or shell tools instead of search_replace. Check diff after EVERY edit for unexpected changes.

whatHappened: Failed to use mandatory MCP tools (codebase.get_context, codebase.check_patterns) before implementing TypeScript fix as required by ROUTER.md
whyItHappened: Jumped straight to implementation without querying knowledge layer first
prevention: ALWAYS call codebase.get_context() BEFORE any implementation. ROUTER.md section 'MANDATORY: Use MCP Tools' explicitly requires this.

message: LLM started deduplication task without calling codebase.get_context() first, despite ROUTER.md lines 16-23 and 59-62 explicitly requiring it. Treated ROUTER.md as reference doc instead of mandatory checklist.
prevention: Add explicit gate in system prompt: 'BEFORE implementing ANYTHING: 1) Check if task mentioned in user query 2) If yes, CALL codebase.get_context() 3) Review returned patterns 4) Proceed only after acknowledging patterns'. Make MCP tool call MANDATORY, not optional.
reflection: ROUTER.md positioned as 'attached context' rather than 'executable requirements'. No enforcement mechanism to block work until MCP tools called. LLM prioritized speed over process compliance.

message: prompt-cache.ts used process.cwd() for path resolution, which returns project root when launched via .mcp.json with absolute path. Static context files weren't being loaded.
prevention: Always use fileURLToPath(import.meta.url) + path.dirname() for path resolution in ESM modules launched via absolute paths
reflection: Developer assumed script would be run from ai_dev_utils/mcp directory. Didn't account for .mcp.json launching with absolute path from any working directory.

message: Added Edge Cases (3 tests) and Error Handling (4 tests) describe blocks
resolution: Fixed by adding 7 tests covering concurrent calls, boundary conditions, hash failures, mismatched hashes, corrupted hashes, db failures

message: Violation was stale - file already has all 4 paths: Happy Path, Sad Path, Edge Cases, Error Path describe blocks
resolution: No action needed - coverage was already complete when violation was logged

message: Vitest workspace glob 'packages/*' and 'packages-oss/*' matched non-package files (vercel-entry.ts, README.md, SECURITY.md), causing config loading failures. Also missing @snapback/engine alias for subpath imports.
prevention: 1) Vitest projects glob must exclude non-package files (like vercel-entry.ts, README.md). 2) Use explicit package paths for packages-oss instead of glob. 3) Add @snapback/engine to vitest-config aliases for subpath imports.
reflection: Glob patterns for vitest projects were too broad, matching any file in the directory including standalone TS and MD files. The engine package was missing from the centralized vitest-config aliases.

message: Created test file in `apps/vscode/src/ui/VitalsIntegration.test.ts` instead of `apps/vscode/test/ui/VitalsIntegration.test.ts`. Vitest config pattern is `test/**/*.test.ts`, so test was ignored by runner.
prevention: Always check vitest.config.ts test.include pattern before creating test files. For VS Code extension: MUST use `test/` directory, NOT `src/`. Add to pre-flight checklist: verify test file location matches config pattern.
reflection: Did not verify vitest.config.ts test.include pattern before creating test file. Assumed co-locating tests with source files would work (common in some projects, but not this codebase).

message: Left 25 placeholder tests with `expect(true).toBe(true); // TODO: Implement` without implementing them. Tests passed but provided zero value - they tested nothing.
prevention: 1. After any test file modification, grep for `expect(true).toBe(true)` and `TODO` in test files. 2. Add to verification checklist: 'No placeholder assertions remain'. 3. Treat passing tests with TODO comments as failures.
reflection: 1. Focused on fixing failing tests (VitalsIntegration) without auditing all related tests. 2. Tests with `expect(true).toBe(true)` pass silently - no failure signal. 3. No verification step to check for TODO/placeholder assertions after test run.

message: 200+ console.log statements found across production code (168 in vscode/src, 32 in engine/src). ValidationPipeline pre-commit hook will now catch new additions.
prevention: Replace with structured logger. Do not add new console.log to production code.

message: Found 18+ console.log() statements in apps/web/modules/marketing production code, violating C-007 constraint. Examples: analytics.ts (lines 23, 38, 48), web-vitals.ts (lines 63-65, 177, 192), performance-monitor.tsx (line 52).
prevention: Add codebase:check_patterns to pre-commit hook and run ValidationPipeline before commits. Replace all console.log with logger from @snapback/infrastructure.
reflection: C-007 constraint exists and ValidationPipeline has detection logic at packages/intelligence/src/validation/layers/index.ts:348, but ValidationPipeline is not integrated into pre-commit hook. No automated enforcement prevents console.log from being committed.

message: Found 16+ instances of (session as any) type casts across apps/web, primarily in account settings, billing, and admin pages. Examples: settings/layout.tsx (line 27), billing/page.tsx (line 46), admin/layout.tsx (line 25). Also found (organization as any) casts.
prevention: Create apps/web/types/session.ts with SessionUser type from BetterAuth. Import proper types instead of casting to any. Consider enabling noExplicitAny: 'warn' in biome.json or adding ValidationPipeline check.
reflection: Session and organization types not properly imported from BetterAuth/Supabase. biome.json has noExplicitAny intentionally disabled (line 14). No automated detection prevents type safety bypasses from being committed.

message: Committed type safety fixes (session types, : any replacements) without running ROUTER.md verification gates (lines 886-898). Trusted lefthook pre-commit hooks as sufficient validation, but did not verify TypeScript compilation passes or run manual testing.
prevention: MANDATORY: Before ANY commit, run the complete verification checklist from ROUTER.md lines 886-898. Cannot skip type-check even if lefthook passes. Add to memory: 'Lefthook ≠ Verification Gates. Always run: 1) pnpm type-check --filter=[package], 2) pnpm test --filter=[package], 3) grep for placeholders, 4) manual testing, 5) user validation'
reflection: Assumed lefthook validation-pipeline was equivalent to running full type-check. Did not follow the explicit checklist: 1) TypeScript compilation passes, 2) Unit tests pass, 3) No placeholder tests, 4) Manual testing, 5) User validation. User correctly identified this gap with 'did you build/test everything first?'

message: Imported @snapback/infrastructure logger (which uses pino/thread-stream Node.js dependencies) in client-side React components. This caused Next.js build failures because Turbopack tried to bundle Node.js-only code (test files, LICENSE files) for the browser. The "use client" directive was also malformed as ("use client") instead of "use client";
prevention: Before importing @snapback/infrastructure logger: 1) Check for "use client" directive at top of file 2) For client components/hooks, use console.* methods instead 3) Only use structured logger in server-side code (API routes, server components, backend services) 4) When doing bulk replacements, filter files by context (server vs client) 5) Ensure "use client" is always line 1 with proper syntax "use client"; not ("use client")
reflection: Bulk replacement of console.log with logger.* across the codebase without checking if files were client-side code. The automated replacement didn't distinguish between server-side and client-side contexts. Additionally, the "use client" directive was moved below imports, breaking React's requirement that it must be the first line.

message: Created two new documentation files (dx-configuration-audit.md, dx-configuration-checklist.md) instead of updating existing ai_dev_utils/ files as instructed
prevention: Always read system reminders and user instructions carefully. User specified to update @ROUTER.md path (ai_dev_utils/), not create new documentation files in docs/. Should have updated patterns/codebase-patterns.md and CONSTRAINTS.md instead.
reflection: Did not carefully read the user's clarification that they meant the @ROUTER.md path (ai_dev_utils/). Jumped to creating new docs despite system reminder saying 'DO NOT proactively create documentation files'

message: Implemented API procedures and dashboard component tests without running ROUTER.md pre-flight checks and verification gates. Completed 6 test files (973 lines), 2 API procedures (346 lines), and updates to routers/exports. However, skipped calling codebase.start_task() before implementation and did not run verification gates (TypeScript compilation, test suite execution) after completing code changes.
prevention: MANDATORY: Before ANY code changes, run codebase.start_task({ task, files, keywords }) to bundle context + learnings. After implementation, ALWAYS execute verification gates: 1) pnpm type-check --filter=[package], 2) pnpm test --filter=[package] --run, 3) grep for placeholders, 4) manual testing. Mark task complete only after all gates pass. Record in memory: 'start_task + implementation + verify_gates = complete task'."
reflection: Focused on implementation delivery and assumed ROUTER.md workflow was optional. Did not treat ROUTER.md as mandatory pre-flight requirement (lines 16-26). Trusted that compilation would catch errors and did not proactively run gates before completion.

message: Declared implementation complete without running build/typecheck - resulted in 15 TypeScript errors
prevention: ALWAYS run pnpm build or pnpm typecheck before declaring any code complete. Never celebrate until verified.
reflection: Rushed to completion summary without verifying code compiles. Relied on IDE diagnostics for import errors but didn't run full build to catch type mismatches.
